channel.title: Resourcehip Blog
channel.description: Insights, buying guides, and sustainability analysis from Resourcehip.

item.title: Generic vs Verified Ratings: Why the Gap Exists, and What Closes It
item.description: Every Resourcehip rating is one of two types. Generic ratings rate the category. Verified ratings rate a specific evidenced product. The gap between them is the headroom — the space where a manufacturer can demonstrably outperform the assumed default.

item.title: How to Choose a Sustainable Air Fryer (UK Buyer's Guide)
item.description: Air fryers cook efficiently but most are designed to be unrepairable and short-lived. The category baseline scores 2.9/10. The ceiling scores 7.1. Here is what closes that gap.

item.title: How to Choose a Sustainable Coffee Pod Machine (UK Buyer's Guide)
item.description: Most pod-machine sustainability conversations focus on the pods. The machine itself matters too. Category baseline: 2.6/10. Ceiling: 7.2. Here is what closes the gap.

item.title: How to Choose a Sustainable Cordless Drill (UK Buyer's Guide)
item.description: A cordless drill is a multi-decade tool with a five-year battery. The category baseline scores 2.5/10. The ceiling scores 6.7. Here is what closes the gap, and why the battery system matters more than the drill.

item.title: How to Choose a Sustainable Electric Toothbrush (UK Buyer's Guide)
item.description: Sealed handles, lithium and cobalt batteries, no spare parts. Category baseline 2.0/10. Ceiling 6.5. Here is what closes that gap and what to check before you buy.

item.title: How to Choose a Sustainable Hair Dryer (UK Buyer's Guide)
item.description: A hair dryer is one of the few small appliances whose motor genuinely outlasts the rest of the product. Category baseline 2.6/10. Ceiling 6.5. Here is what closes that gap.

item.title: How to Choose a Sustainable Kettle (UK Buyer's Guide)
item.description: Kettles have the highest baseline HIP score in the small-appliance set. The category ceiling is Silver-adjacent. Here is what closes the gap between a 2-year disposable and a 12-year durable.

item.title: How to Choose a Sustainable Portable Speaker (UK Buyer's Guide)
item.description: Speaker drivers can last decades. The lithium-ion battery typically does not. Category baseline 2.0/10. Ceiling 6.4. Here is what closes that gap.

item.title: How to Choose a Sustainable Robot Vacuum (UK Buyer's Guide)
item.description: A robot vacuum is the only category where software lifespan can brick the hardware. Category baseline 2.3/10. Ceiling 6.6. Here is what closes that gap.

item.title: How to Choose a Sustainable Smartphone (UK Buyer's Guide)
item.description: The category baseline scores 1.8/10. The verified ceiling scores 6.4. Here is what closes that gap, and what to look for as a UK buyer.

item.title: How to Choose a Sustainable Pair of Wireless Earbuds (UK Buyer's Guide)
item.description: Wireless earbuds are the only category where even the verified ceiling falls short of the Standard HIP Mark. Baseline 1.8/10. Ceiling 5.9. Here is what that means for buyers.

item.title: What is the HIP Score? A Plain-English Explainer
item.description: The HIP Score is a 0–10 rating of how a product is built, sourced, and recovered — not how it performs in use. Here is what each of the seven dimensions measures, and why the score moves where it does.

item.title: What is the Regenerative Index? Why HIP Has a Dimension That Goes Negative
item.description: The Regenerative Index is the only HIP dimension with a negative scale — from -10 to +10. Here is why it exists, what each of the five tiers means, and why almost every consumer product today is Depleting.

item.title: Why Your Kettle's Longevity Matters
item.description: Choosing a longer-lasting kettle reduces landfill waste and cuts your lifetime costs — here's what the HIP score reveals.
